denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/17/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100665196          Group #: GRP-1084
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-115529
Claim #: CLM-2026-192453
Date of Service (requested): 03/28/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 03/30/2026 for the requested service.: Letrozole ovulation induction
Diagnosis (as submitted): [REDACTED]
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM104 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM104 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Letrozole ovulation induction with an out-of-network provider is not covered without a gap exception. In-network alternatives are available.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with out of network no authorization. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100665196) and reference PA-2026-115529. 
Funding: This determination applies under your employer's self-funded group arrangement (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Polycystic ovary syndrome with infertility (E28.2). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. She has been treated under commercial coverage with Letrozole ovulation induction recommended as the next appropriate step. Anovulatory cycles; failed clomiphene 100 mg ×3 cycles. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that out of network no authorization bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
